content: Pandemic preparedness assessment tools: Joint External Evaluation (JEE) scores IHR core capacities across 19 technical areas; SPAR (State Party Self-Assessment Annual Reporting) tracks progress; Global Health Security Index benchmarks national capabilities. Persistent gaps: only ~30% of countries achieved high JEE scores; financing shortfalls estimated at $10-30 billion annually needed globally; weak primary healthcare systems limit detection; limited manufacturing capacity in low-income regions; inadequate data sharing mechanisms. Post-COVID reforms proposed: new pandemic treaty, strengthened WHO authority, permanent financing mechanism, technology transfer hubs, and equity frameworks for countermeasure access.
source: WHO JEE reports, Global Health Security Index, Independent Panel for Pandemic Preparedness and Response
